tokens: (Long Pause) Okay; I got a tone.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Pause) One pound, cabin; 0.6 pounds on the cabin.
Half a pound in the cabin.
You might be able to get the door open partly.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Better let her drop a little more.
It's a pretty heavy pull there.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You got a lot of surface area on that hatch.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Okay, there's a quarter of a pound.
It should be almost zero now.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, we're showing a tenth (0.1) of a pound right now.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Would you hold it (the hatch) for me, please?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Slight Pause) Okay, I(Ôve) got it now.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Pause) We wait for the water flag.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Just barely see them over there.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Getting a water flag clear here in a minute.
(Pause) Okay my PGA is getting down to about usable pressure of 4.3 now.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We'll be able to work in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The Caution and Warning status is good.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're showing your feedwater pressure going up (in response to the buildup of the ice layer in the sublimator).
You ought to be in business shortly.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Your water flag about to clear?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Al's pressure is rising now; it ought to clear momentarily.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Okay, Al's water flag is clear.

tokens: Forward hatch the rest of the way open.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Get your leg right up there.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay; can you reach everything now?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Let's see, I got...That ought to help you.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Wait; you're going to have to get your PLSS down a little; roll toward me.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Get your head down as soon as you can.
Wait a minute, let me get your antenna; hold it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You'll have to get mine when I come out.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Let me get over here on the other side so I can get to it.
(Long Pause) I'm hung up on something, Al.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Now I'm standing by for the LEC (as per his
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: While he's working on the LEC, let me comment that it certainly is a stark place here at Fra Mauro.
I think it's made all the more stark by the fact that the sky is completely black.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, I have the conveyor now.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: We can see you coming down the ladder right now.
It looks like you're about on the bottom step.
Not bad for an old man.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: (referring to himself in the third person) Al is on the surface.
And it's been a long way, but we're here.
(Pause) Well, I can see the reason we have a tilt is because we landed on a slope.
The landing gear struts appear to be about evenly depressed.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'm moving around (but not away from the ladder), getting familiar with the surface.
The surface on which the forward (west) footpad landed is extremely soft.
As a matter of fact, it's in a small depression.
The soil is so soft that it comes all the way to the top of the footpad; it even folded over the sides to some degree.
The same is true of the
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Moving out of the TV picture to the north) Okay, we'll move on over.
Take a look at Fra Mauro...(correcting himself) take a look at Cone Crater, I should say, which is right where it should be, and is a very impressive sight.
You can see the boulders near the rim (garbled under McCandless)...
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: You are Go for two-man EVA.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And, continuing, we can see the boulders on the rim.
It looks as though we have a good traverse route up to the top of Cone.
I can see Cone Ridge going along to the north.
(Pause) I'm moving over to adjust the MESA.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'm finishing up my circuit breaker check.
Will be ready to go out shortly.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We can see you coming down the ladder, now.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And it's very great to be coming down.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: That last one is a long one.
(Pause) (Hopping back up to the first rung of the ladder, just barely) Ascent check.
A little push and just spring right up.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I guess we got there with those lightweight (PLSS) units.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Have you released the MET, yet?
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: The MET is finally clear of the MESA.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Al, I'm going to come over.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: ...if I go around the corner or something.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (I'll) just drop this baby (meaning the MET) over here (in the +Y footpad).
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Long Pause) It is bright (looking) up-Sun, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: If you'll stop here a minute, I'll get your (OPS) antenna out.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, Houston, the MESA (meaning MET) has been stowed on the
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And going back to adjust the MESA.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Mobility is very great under this crushing one-sixth-g load, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And looking at Cone Crater, where Al was looking a short time ago, it doesn't appear there is going to be any trouble getting the MET up Cone Crater.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We knew the troops on the ground would be glad to hear that.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And here comes the lens cap.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: (Long Pause) Let me give you a hand, and we'll get it done.

tokens: Okay, the lens cap is going on now, Houston.
While we set up the tripod...
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: LMP is 3.75 psi; reading 85 percent (oxygen remaining); all flags Go.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, CDR is 81 percent, 3.75, no flags, Min cooling.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: While Al's getting that television, I'll go ahead and get my contingency sample; get it out of the way.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The contingency sample is being taken about 25 feet in the 0100 position of the LM, adjacent to about a 5-foot crater.
I'll identify it for you later.

transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Would you verify the lens is still capped.

transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Go ahead and pull it out.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Let me get this contingency sample folded up.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, Houston, the lens cap is off.
We're aiming for the general area of the MESA.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Al, could you pull the rest of this cable out away from the MESA here...
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (To McCandless) Okay; you've got about 30-foot zoom.

tokens: I think you can zoom in a little more.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And on the f-stop, Al, we'd like to stop it down one additional stop.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: It's going from 22 to 44, and I'll zoom it in to 40.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Hold the zoom there, and the position looks good, also.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: (Long Pause) Is the f-stop satisfactory, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: See if you can stop it down a little more.
Run the diaphragm ring up against the stop, there.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: It's right up against the stop.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Request you go to peak control.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Now, we'd like to open it up to f/22.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Okay, this is the next adjustment to f/22.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Would you confirm that you're at f/22 now?
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Okay, I'm confirming that I'm in peak and I'm at f/22.
(Long Pause) And while we're waiting for the television adjustment, the 2:30 position (at) approximately 50 feet, where the camera is, is slightly uphill.
We see that the LM did, in fact, land on sort of a downslope...
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: ...It seems to be almost a basin.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: We'd like to go back to average and f/44; stop it down all the way, and then leave it there.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Okay, this is the last adjustment.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Roger, and back the zoom out to about 35.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And back the zoom, out to 35.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, pressing on (to deploy) the S-band antenna.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Again continuing; the soil is very fine here.
Very fine grained; and, as we mentioned before, there are very few samples of any size at all.
Mostly hand-sample size and rocks of generally under 2 inches or less.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Houston, as you can see, the SRC table is deployed.
ETB is emptied; and I'm putting LiOH canisters in it now.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And you did leave the contingency sample on the ladder (as per checklist)?
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Yes, it looks as though we've landed in a fairly rough place.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Evidenced by the fact that you dug your front landing gear into a hole.
I have the SWC (Solar Wind Collector) out and starting now to deploy it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Am I still in your field-of-view, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, Al is bringing the S-band antenna around (the ladder to a spot 20 feet out from the LM at the 3 o'clock position).
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: At 114 plus 53 plus 48 GET (114:13:45 transcript time).
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And, Houston, the LR-cubed is coming off.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: If you would, give us some commentary on how the legs go into the surface.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Okay, the legs are in the surface approximately 1 inch, I would say.
Appear to be fairly equal all the way around; perhaps the leg to the left is in an inch and a half.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: We were driving more at force penetration.
And did you meet any rocks or anything like that?
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: I didn't attempt to run any kind of an experiment...
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Commenting on Ed's stride as he heads out toward Shepard) Looks more like a kangaroo.

tokens: I should have more cable than that.
(Pause as Ed goes back to the MESA to free the cable)
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Having turned to see how far he is from the end of the cable) Okay.
How are we with respect to the cable.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Coming out with the cable) I'm afraid you're too far away.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Well, I wanted to get maximum length; but I'll moving on in.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Yeah, I want to do that, too (that is, get the S-Band as far out as possible).
(Pause) This is all (the cable) I've got, Al.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Right about in here anywhere will probably do it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We'll have to put it right here to get it level.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: If you want to stand clear, we'll deploy the antenna (umbrella).
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: It's hung up at the top.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Can you tilt it over toward me without dropping it...I'll get it unhung for you.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: All kinds of freebies in today's simulation.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: We've got the boys in the Backroom working overtime.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Let me get on the glass.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (I'll) turn it to the left a little more.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (I'll) put some weight on it because that changes the whole deal.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: It'll tip over here in a minute.
transcript.speaker: MItchell

tokens: I'll just put it back down again.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I think my PLSS - my OPS - is hitting it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Let me back it off a minute.
And move this a little bit.
(Pause) Okay, that's about it for azimuth.
I'll come down a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Let me just check and see if that's the way we want to go.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, come on down a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, I have the Earth centered.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Boresighted the Earth, dark side and all.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The S-band antenna has been erected and aligned and the cable has been attached.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And I'll go back in to switch.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And we'd like to get an EMU status report as you go by.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: I have no flags; I'm still in minimum flow; and I'm comfortable.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'm reading 3.75, about 75 percent on the O2, no flags, minimum cooling, and I'm very comfortable, too.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And, I guess (you have put - or will put - the) contingency sample into the ETB (Equipment Transfer Bag).
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: (Stamping his feet at the top of the ladder) Hey, Bruce.
Is any appreciable dust flying off these boots?
I'd like not to take all that dirt in there.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I didn't notice any on the TV.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And, Houston, I'm back in the LM.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Without a great deal of problem.
I'm getting ready to switch to Lunar Stay.
Give me a call, and, if I don't hear you in about 30 seconds, we'll go back.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You're Go to switch to Lunar Stay.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Okay, you're on the erectable antenna.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And how are you reading us?
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: I'm ready for the ETB most anytime.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And did the contingency sample get in there?
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Wouldn't never do for us to leave that one behind, Bruce.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (From the foot of the ladder) While Ed is loading up the ETB...(Listens) While Ed is loading up the ETB (with film mags and cameras), (as per checklist) I'll describe the general landing site.
We are, in fact, in a low area.
(Looking east and south) There seems to be a general swale or a wide valley between the Triplet Craters and the Doublet Craters.
And we are on the downhill side at this particular point.
(Turning slowly toward the west) It levels off at a lower elevation to the left of the LM (south), approximately 15 feet lower there, and then it starts back up to the rim of Doublet.
It's a very uneven landing area here.
And, of course, like all of the sections of the Moon, it's pockmarked by (a) tremendous amount of craters.

The surface here, as we pointed out, is mostly fines and I hate to discuss any kind of lineations here in the immediate vicinity of the LM, because I can see very definite indications of the radial dust pattern caused by the descent engine.
And I don't see any other lineal pattern, as such, right here in the area.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: There are, oh, perhaps half a dozen fairly large rocks at the 1 o'clock position from the LM.
(Uncertain) Perhaps they're ejected from Cone, although they don't seem to have any particular ray pattern.
They probably are ejected from Doublet, since they appear to be closer to Doublet than they do Triplet.
They are a lighter gray in material - excuse me - the material is lighter gray in color and I'm certain that we'll get some of those samples on the way back from our ALSEP deployment.

(Now facing east, apparently trying to keep his eyes in the shadow of the LM) It's very difficult to assess any kind of stratigraphy in Cone right now, looking back at it, because we're looking into the Sun at a low Sun angle, and it's just not the right direction to view that crater for looking for stratigraphy.
But there certainly are boulders on it.
From here, it looks as though they are at least 20 feet in diameter perhaps, at least the ones we can see here in the western slope.
They appear to be grouped fairly close to the rim of the crater and not too many large boulders on down the sides of the slopes, the outside rim.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Here again, it looks as though the LM was traveling slowly forward and slowly to the right.
As you'll see from the photographs, that's the direction of the landing gear probes, as they are bent.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, Ed, how're you (doing) up there?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'm about ready to start down with it now.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Pause) The ISA came loose from its straps and is being a great major headache.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Before you start transferring, you want to verify contents in the ETB.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Okay, let me give you a call on them, Bruce.
I put in one black-and-white camera...(correcting himself) television camera, two Hasselblads, one TDS, two 16-millimeter Mags, and two maps.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Did you get the 16-millimeter camera with Mag attached?
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Guess we kind of need that one.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Yeah, that's the one that's supposed to photograph you coming down the ladder!
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Pause) All of the contingency...The disposal containers just fell out on the floor.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: With respect to the erosion pattern, directly under the engine bell there is very little erosion.
Most of the erosion occurs about 3 feet to the southeast of the current location of the bell.
That's probably where the thrust was when the engine was cut off.
And the LM slowly drifted to the northwest from there.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: As perhaps you can see from your (TV) camera Houston, the view off to the south is undulating hills.
And I would estimate that (highest) hill back there to the south is, oh, perhaps 100 feet higher than we are.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I am ready to bring this (ETB) down.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Backing up to get the line taut) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, just a second here, we'll get a little more tension.
(Pause) Put a little more tension, please.
Okay, coming over the steps now.
Clear of the steps; and I'll take it down slowly.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Do you have it in hand?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Just hold it right there for a minute.
Okay, ease it down a couple of feet.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'll get a picture of you.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Give Al a few seconds to get the camera (unloaded before Ed comes back out)...(Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: (Long Pause) Okay, lens cover's coming off (the 16-mm movie camera).
(Long Pause as Al moves out of the TV picture north of the ladder) (To Ed) Okay, come on down.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Mitchell and McCandless: (Recorded in Houston simultaneously) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Unknown

tokens: Give me a mark, Al, when you start using film.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: I just started using film now.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Long Pause) Tell me when I hit the bottom step.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I wanted to miss the LR-cubed (which is in the ladder footpad).
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, we're right on the timeline.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, I'll take the (16-mm) camera while you get the flag set up.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Want to go off to the left over there by the SWC?
(So that Houston can) see it on television.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (It will) be the best place, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Long Pause) The camera was on 1/60th (of a second shutter speed) and I hope it got bumped there.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: No, that's where it's supposed to be for you.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Aim my (16 mm) camera out there at about the right spot.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Right over there on the rise?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Let me point a little bit further around that way.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Get out there in the sunlight, I think, with it.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: The flag is going off the (field-of-view of the TV) camera to the right.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Al, we're not going...That's too far around.
We're not going to be able to get it with the 16(-mm camera), now.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Well, we can put it (pause) right down here close by, if he wants.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: ...I think it would look a lot better if you could bring it over closer towards the TV.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Put it right here in front of us, Al.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Yeah, maybe on the TV camera side of the LM shadow.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Get my (16 mm) camera going here.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Thinking that it's Ed who is erecting the flag) Ed, now you're going off-camera to the right.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: (To Ed) Take a picture this way (facing the 16-mm camera) and then we'll swing it around so they can see it in the television.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, let me turn it around a little (garbled) here.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I think I'm still too close to you, Al.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Look out for the LM leg.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And when you're finished (having his picture taken), you can flop it around so they can see it a little better on the TV.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, and which magazine are you using?
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Indianapolis, Indiana; but that was my line.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Got your feet in the TV cable, Al.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The S-band cable both...Got them both.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Give me a good orientation for the flag.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Just turn it broadside to the TV camera with the field to the TV camera right; that is, 180 out from that would be better.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: (To Houston) You got 25 on the (Hasselblad) Mag.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (To Ed) Okay, that's good on the flag.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: You have about 3 minutes remaining on that magazine, Claremont, California.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Okay, I'm going to press on out for the TV pan, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And, while Ed is doing that, Al is going to proceed with photographing the landing gear...
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: ...and all the features about the LM.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: For my first sector for pan, I'll point it (the TV) a little bit more to the south.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We want to go to a zoom of 25 on this.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: You're zoom of 25; focus pointing out toward infinity.
(Pause; no answer) Houston, this is Ed.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The horizon is about two-thirds of the way up from the bottom of the tube.
The flag is over near the left-hand corner of the field-of-view.
And that little rise is sort of centered.
(Pause) With the small crater off to the left.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Okay, that's just about where I wanted it.
The far horizon, Bruce, is a ridge that seems to run around this bowl that we're sitting in...It appears to be a ridge.
It runs down from what we called "
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: ...a rise that's shown on the map.
The craters are on the map.
Since I don't have it handy, it - I'll have to give you the coordinates later, but I think you already know them.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: If you're going to spend several seconds describing each of these locations here, after the camera steadies out you might just as well zoom out a ways, and we'll pick up some features at random on higher magnification, and zoom back in when you go onto the next 45-degree sector.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: All right, I've moved around to the next sector now.
And it's looking down over what we used to call Clover Leaf, although it's not obvious from here what the clover leaf was.
There is a fairly significant crater about 250 to 300 yards out.
I'll try to bring it in for you.
Can you see it out there, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Keep zooming, if you've got any left.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: That crater is kind of in a low spot, but it's not the lowest spot in this dip that we're in.
The lowest spot we will pick up in our next sector.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, bring her (the zoom) back in.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: However, I will shoot across it because you won't be able to see it.
Now, another sector to the right.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The deepest part, I guess, of what we were calling Clover Leaf before, although I did not realize how deep that depression was and I still don't quite...
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, zoom out while you're talking.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Can't quite get the relief in my mind, because, it is so different than what I expected.
Where you're looking at now - this deep part - is to the south of Doublet, and it's probably 75 to 100 feet below where we are.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Noting that the horizon is now near the bottom of the frame) Okay, you're aimed up a little high.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: It rises up on the far side to (an elevation) above us.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Say, Ed, you don't need to stop talking when I talk if you can do both at once.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: I have a little trouble listening to you and talking at you, too.
And coming around through the west-northwest (and stopping just as his shadow goes out the lower left corner of the picture); and you should be able to see, in the distance, Doublet Crater.
And I've lost it now because of the Sun angle, but it's just about on the near horizon.
I'm sorry; there are three mounds, three ridges.
The nearest one, the ridge that Doublet is on, and then the far horizon.
And I'm bringing it on out for you.
Doublet is on the second hill that you see.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Roger, we can see the ridges, and I...
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: ...can see a crater that probably is Doublet.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: We'll zoom back in and move on around, and I think Al's about to finish up his task over there.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Ed, I just wonder how come McCandless has the audacity to presume that we're wrong about Doublet Crater.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, Bruce, I'm coming around one more sector.
(Pause) I'm going to move it just a little bit more (and) you should be able to see the four or five rocks I was talking about in my discussion before we got out of the LM.
Now, I'll zoom in on those if I may.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, now point the camera down a degree or 2.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: You might come right a degree or so.
I see the small rocks off to the right.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: ...What's that object in profile on the horizon?
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: I mentioned a quadruplet chain of craters...(Listens)
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: ...They're right here in front of me...(listens) Okay.
The quadruplet chain of craters starts right here in front of me; well, it's halfway between the rock and myself...
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Garbled) across here, now, there's quite a few...(Listens) Let me zoom back in again.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: There's the south quadruplet crater, and then there's the next one and the next one and the largest one.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Pause) Okay, we probably better go back to zoom 25 and press on with the panorama.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: And I'm looking almost due north now.
I'll swing back around and pan for my rocks.
There's the rocks we were looking at.
Panning slowly to the north, you can now see the undulations, the ridges that I was talking about.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: There's not a level portion out here that's more than a few square meters.
And you can see at least three ridges between us and the horizon.
Now, I'll zoom in out here once more.
Let you see at close-hand what's out there.
Another pile of rocks (and) more ridges.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You need a gunsight on this thing.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The horizon is about one quarter of the way up.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Al is finished with the documentation; (and the frame) counter (is reading) 110.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And, Ed, a frame or two ago it looked like one of those rocks was split right down the middle.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: I...It may be; it may look like it from there.
We'll go by there later on.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Let's press on with the TV panorama.
(Pause) We're about 2 minutes behind timeline at this point, Ed.
And you're looking at sky again.
Okay, Ed, we're recording all this on video tape so that it only takes a relatively brief period of time looking at the scene that we can play it back, frame at a time, later on.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Don't look like Ed's reading anybody.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Ed, this is Houston; we're not reading you.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Okay, Bruce, now you are; I hit the transmit switch to off.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The horizon that you see in this view is the north flank leading up to Cone Crater.
It's probably over a mile away, a mile and a half away.
I'll give a quick zoom in on it.
And then I can't go any closer to the Sun right now.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, you're looking at sky again.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Okay, we're at the time to deploy the MET, Ed, if you want to swing it on back around.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Bruce, what was the zoom setting you wanted...
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: ...it at about 45 there; we'd like to get the flag in at the right extremity and the
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Back it out about to 40 on the zoom.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Al and Ed, if we could get you both in the field-of-view there for a minute, we've got a message for you.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: Why don't you take a pair and let me pass a message to you.
transcript.speaker: Slayton

tokens: We were very pleased a few minutes ago to receive a phone call here in Mission Control from President Nixon.
He asked me to extend to you and to Stu his best congratulations.
He said that, like millions of people all over the world, he is an astronaut watcher at this time.
The picture is coming in very well at the White House, he said.
The President said he knew how many thousands of people had worked on this mission without whom men would not be walking safely on the moon.
He asked that I wish the Apollo entire team well.
The President said he was proud of you and proud of them.
He sent you a wire just before the flight wishing you Godspeed, and he wishes you well on your return flight.
The President also asked me to invite you to the White House for dinner and to spend the weekend at Camp David with your families after the mission is completed.
transcript.speaker: Slayton

tokens: And we appreciate those kind words.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And convey our thanks to the President, please.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I don't think Stu got this, but we'll see he gets it later.
transcript.speaker: Slayton

tokens: Both tires are inflated properly on the MET.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Pause) Let's get the...(Pause) Deploy it while it's still up here.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Well, let's see...We've had visitors again.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Houston, as you can see, the MET is deployed properly.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Looks like it's in good shape.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, if you want to...Leave this right here, I'll move around to put the TV camera on the scientific equipment (SEQ) bay.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: We'd like to get an EMU status report at this point.
transcript.speaker: McCandless

tokens: (As he runs out to get the TV) Okay.
CDR is 3.7 and reading 72 (percent oxygen remaining); no flags.
I'm on low flow (that is, minimum cooling), and in good shape.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And the LMP is 3.7 and reading 67 percent; low flow, (that is), low cooling, and feeling great.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: TV camera (lens) is covered, and proceeding to the rear of the LM to observe the deployment of the ALSEP.
transcript.speaker: Shepard
